7.
Question 7
What is "code" in the context of this course?

1 point

A way to encrypt data during World War II


A password we use to unlock Python features


A sequence of instructions in a programming language


A set of rules that govern the style of programs
A sequence of instructions in a programming language

9.
Question 9
What is the best way to think about a "Syntax Error" while programming?

1 point

The computer is overheating and just wants you to stop to let it cool down


The computer did not understand the statement that you entered


The computer has used GPS to find your location and hates everyone from your town


The computer needs to have its software upgraded
The computer did not understand the statement that you entered.

10.
Question 10
Which of the following is not one of the programming patterns covered in Chapter 1?

1 point

Repeated Steps


Sequential Steps


Conditional Steps


Random steps

Assignment: Write Hello World
You'll complete this item using the course's autograders. Open the tool using the button below.

This course uses a third-party app, Assignment: Write Hello World, to enhance your learning experience. The app will reference basic information like your name and Coursera ID.

As an AI Language Model, I don't have access to external apps or platforms. But if you want to write "Hello, World" in Python, you can do it using the print() function. Here's how:

```
print("Hello, World")
```

When you run this code, it will print the string "Hello, World" to the console.
